game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Kniffel® Klassik:** Kniffel is a turn-based digital dice game where 1-8 players compete to achieve the highest score by rolling five six-sided dice. On each turn, a player gets up to three rolls: they tap a "Roll" button to initiate the first roll, then tap individual dice on the screen to "hold" them before tapping "Roll" again for subsequent re-rolls of the unheld dice. After the third roll, or earlier if satisfied, the player must select one of 13 available scoring categories (e.g., "Three of a Kind," "Full House," "Kniffel/Yahtzee") from a digital score sheet to record their points, with each category usable only once per game. The game progresses through rounds until all 13 scoring categories are filled by every player, at which point the player with the highest cumulative score is declared the winner. Difficulty for AI opponents can be selected from 3 levels (e.g., Easy, Medium, Hard), influencing their strategic scoring decisions, while local multiplayer supports up to 8 players sharing a single device. The UI features clear dice display, tap-to-hold functionality, and a dynamic score sheet, with optional motion sensor input allowing players to shake their device to simulate a dice roll.
game_info.description: Roll the dice and achieve the highest score in this strategic dice game! You have 13 rounds to fill your scorecard with different combinations like Three of a Kind, Full House, or the coveted Kniffel (five of a kind). Each turn, roll up to three times, strategically holding dice between rolls. Choose wisely—each category can only be used once! Compete against AI opponents and aim for the top score.
game_info.controls:
ENTER: Start the game
SPACE: Roll dice / Continue to next turn
Arrow Keys (LEFT/RIGHT): Navigate scorecard categories
Arrow Keys (UP/DOWN): Select dice to hold/unhold
Z: Confirm category selection
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart (return to start screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Basic functionality test. Validates that the game can execute basic actions: rolling dice, holding dice, selecting categories, and progressing through turns without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Execute random valid actions: roll dice when available, randomly hold/unhold dice, and select random available categories. Ensure the game can complete at least 5 turns without crashing.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The game should progress through multiple turns, update scores correctly, and handle all basic interactions without errors. Success is completing 5+ turns with valid state transitions.

TEST_1.expected: The game should progress through multiple turns, update scores correctly, and handle all basic interactions without errors. Success is completing 5+ turns with valid state transitions.

TEST_2.test_description: Optimal win strategy test. Tests if the game can be won by implementing an intelligent scoring strategy that maximizes points and beats AI opponents.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Prioritize high-value categories (Kniffel, Full House, Straights). Hold dice strategically to build valuable combinations. Calculate expected values for each category and choose the best available option. Aim for bonus points in upper section.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The game should complete all 13 rounds with an optimized score above 200 points. The test is successful when achieving GAME_OVER_WIN state by beating all AI opponents.
